title: Aceras hircinum
taxonomy: Aceras hircinum belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly referred to as the orchid family. It is a flowering plant native to southern Europe and parts of Asia. Taxonomically, it is classified as a genus within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Aceras hircum is also classified as a type of orchid that is characterized by its unique, horn-shaped flowers. The species is further classified as Aceras hircinum in the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Aceras hircinum, also known as the Cat's-nose or Cat's tongue orchid, is a perennial plant species in the orchid family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Aceras hircinum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and capsules, to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the digestive system and reproductive health.
